Name: Emberdawn
Summary: Fire platform boss with debuff puddles, high-damage intermissions, and rotating frontal sweeps.
Description:
[Phase 1] Flaming Updraft targets move to the outer rim so their puddles and later Flaming Twisters do not cut the center of the platform.
[Phase 2] During Burning Gale, heal through the raid damage while dodging spawned twisters and the rotating Fire Breath frontals.
[Phase 3] As floor space shrinks, keep safe outer placements disciplined so the next gale cycle still leaves clean lanes.

Name: Derelict Duo
Summary: Two-target undead fight with simultaneous-kill pressure, interrupt checks, and forced line play.
Description:
[Phase 1] Keep both bosses even, because Broken Bond on the survivor becomes unmanageable if one dies early.
[Phase 2] Interrupt Shadow Bolt, dispel or out-range Curse of Darkness, and use crowd control to stop the Dark Entity if the curse cannot be removed.
[Phase 3] When Debilitating Shriek overlaps Heaving Yank, the yanked player runs behind Kalis to stop the group blast while the team keeps both bosses close for cleave.

Name: Commander Kroluk
Summary: Leap-baiting commander fight with fear checks, tank channels, and add-cleave positioning.
Description:
[Phase 1] Designate the furthest player to bait the first Reckless Leap, then let the tank bait the second while everyone dodges Falling Rubble.
[Phase 2] Pair up or stack in small groups for Intimidating Shout so nobody gets feared out of position.
[Phase 3] Tanks use defensives for Rampage, keep spawned adds stacked for cleave, and heal Rallying Bellow plus leap damage before the next sequence.

Name: The Restless Heart
Summary: Arrow-driven finale where the group clears stacks and times aerial dodges to survive wind mechanics.
Description:
[Phase 1] Dodge Arrow Rain while using Turbulent Arrows to remove Squall Leap stacks before they become unmanageable.
[Phase 2] Pre-position for Bullseye Windblast and Billowing Wind so the group does not double up on the same arrow and lose a safe launch point.
[Phase 3] Tanks can use an arrow to cancel Tempest Slash knockback while also clearing their own stacks, and the party should use personals for Bolt Gale.
